name: Charlie Byrd
abstract: Charlie Lee Byrd (September 16, 1925 – December 2, 1999) was an American jazz guitarist who played fingerstyle on a classical guitar. Byrd was best known for his association with Brazilian music, especially bossa nova.

concerts.label: Three Guitars with Charlie Byrd, Herb Ellis, and Barney Kessel
Charlie Byrd, Herb Ellis, and Barney Kessel: Three Guitars
Newport Jazz Festival: Charlie Byrd Trio/ Stan Getz Quintet/ João Gilberto

about_quotes.speaker: Keter Betts
about_quotes.evidence: that’s how we hooked up with Charlie. And we worked at this club for about three months... Nat Adderley and Charlie Byrd and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: all I knew was that he had made a bossa nova record with Charlie Byrd which was getting a lot of record sales, but not music I much related to, so I didn't think much about it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, we had a (inaudible) with them, yes. And we did some interesting things. We redid 'Summer Sequence.' ... And we even redid 'Ebony Concerto.'

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: They bring in a lot of the jazz musicians and soloists. I mean, Marian McPartland has been down there with her trio; Charlie Byrd in there.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Donald Byrd, what's his name, Charlie Byrd went down there and he did some things down there.

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWEE] Charlie Byrd. ... [INTERVIEWEE] But that was Charlie Byrd's date.
